details.text: Derivative: سَبَّحَ is in the past continuous tense meaning: “Has been declaring the perfection”.

- In the beginning of this Verse: “مَّن ذَا الَّذِي يُقْرِضُ اللَّهَ قَرْضًا حَسَنًا” instead of مَنِ الَّذِي is sufficient in Arabic to state “who will be the one who will loan Allah a good loan”. However, Allah adds: “ذَا” stating: “مَّن ذَا الَّذِي” which means “Who is it that will be the one who” and it’s like saying: “Is there anyone (out there)” i.e., who will do that.
Derivative: سُورٍ is a large wall, like those which encircle a city.
Derivative: نَقْتَبِسْ from اِقْتَبَسَ – اِقْتِباس and it means to borrow a flame i.e., نَقْتَبِسْ means can we have some or a portion of (your light).
Derivative: الْتَمِسُوا from اِلْتَمَسَ which means طَلَبَ “to seek something out or to look and search for it”.

Derivative: تَرَبَّصْتُمْ from تَرَبَّصَ means to procrastinate, to wait and see, or to wait around for an opportunity to do something. تَرَبَّصْتُمْ means you procrastinated and said to yourself something like: “You can wait, you have time, there’s no need to rush and you don’t need to make changes or do something right now”. Or it can mean: “That you lay in wait for harm or calamities to afflict the believers”.
Derivative: فَتَنتُمْ from فَتَنَ – فِتْنَة. فِتْنَة (Fitnah) means a trial, test or temptation. The verb فَتَنَ means to subject oneself to فِتْنَة (Fitnah) i.e., to temptations and trouble.

Derivative: قَسَتْ from قَسَا – قَسْوَة which means to become hard, harsh and remorseless. It is only used in the Qur’an in relation to hearts becoming hard i.e., devoid of feeling or obdurate in the sense that they are not affected by argument and are obstinate.
Derivative: يَأْنِ from أَنَى which means for a time to draw close. Here, the meaning is: “Has not the time come for …?” or “Is it not high time for …?”

- “And what (مَا) He revealed (نَزَلَ) from (مِن) the truth (الحَقِّ)” – The words مِنَ الْحَقِّ in addition to meaning “of or from the truth” it can also imply what He revealed that is “deserving and worthy” i.e., to be given time and attention, in the case where it has a right (حَقّ - Haqq) over you.

Derivative: الصِّدِّيقُونَ – The difference between صَادِق (Saadiq – The truthful and one who affirms the truth) and صِدِّيق (Siddeeq – The one who affirms the truth without hesitation i.e., at the forefront of affirming the truth). صِدِّيق is in the فِعِّيل form which is rare in the Arabic language and is from the forms of مُبَالَغَة (Mubaalaghah – A more powerful form with greater emphasis).
Derivative: الْكُفَّارَ here means the farmers who bury seeds. الْكُفَّارَ with the meaning of disbelievers comes from them burying or concealing something i.e., the truth and Imaan.
Derivative: يَهِيجُ means to mature i.e., ripen and grow to its extent.

Derivative: حُطَامًا refers to the left-over fragments of something that has been destroyed like rubble or detritus (dead crumbled leaves, stalks etc.). It is something that you would step upon without care or concern. It implies worthlessness and having no value.
Derivative: غَيْثٍ refers to rain which comes when it is needed at its opportune time.
Derivative: أَنَّمَا which combines أَنَّ and مَا indicates to الحَصْر (Al-Hasr - Exclusivity) and can be translated as only i.e., it is only that and nothing other or more than it.

- الْحَيَاةُ الدُّنْيَا is understood to mean “The worldly life” in the case where the دُنْيا refers to the life of this world. However, دُنْيا linguistically as a superlative adjective is the feminine of أَدْنَى which means “the lowest or most inferior” i.e., in comparison to that of the hereafter.
Derivative: السَّمَاءِ is usually translated as the heavens or sky, but in Arabic السَّمَاءِ (As-Samaa’) refers to everything that is above you and is an unrestricted term.
Derivative: نَبْرَأَ is from بَرَأَ and it means to create like خَلَقَ (Khalaqa – To create and bring into being). نَبْرَأَ means “We created” or “We brought into being”.
Derivative: فَخُورٍ means extremely proud i.e., in oneself.

Derivative: مُخْتَالٍ comes from the Arabic word خَيَال which means imagination. It refers to someone who has imaginary sense of himself and self-conceit, where the person has a favourable opinion of himself and his abilities or accomplishments.
Derivative: الْحَمِيدُ – The ultimately praised who is praised in Himself without the need to be praised by others.
Derivative: الْغَنِيُّ – The ultimately wealthy and free of need, the one who is no need of your money.
Derivative: بَأْسٌ refers to the power and might to engage in warfare.
Derivative: الْمِيزَانَ is a balance or scale used for weighing. It is used as a reference for principles of justice.
Derivative: قَفَّيْنَا – قَفَا – يَقْفُو means to follow right after somebody. قَفَّى means to make to follow after somebody or something or to follow up.
Derivative: رَهْبَانِيَّةً is monastic or monk life. One that cuts one off from the rest of life’s affairs and focuses on individualistic spiritual practise.

هُوَ الْأَوَّلُ وَالْآخِرُ وَالظَّاهِرُ وَالْبَاطِنُ

- Usually when Allah mentions His Names, He does not separate them with a وَ (and). This is unique because they include opposites or antonyms. الْأَوَّلُ (The First) and الْآخِرُ (The Last), الظَّاهِرُ (The Evident/Obvious) and ٱلْبَاطِنُ (The Hidden).

Derivative: الْأُمُورُ is the plural of أَمْر (a matter or affair). It refers to all matters and includes actions and intentions of His slaves and also all decisions and judgements in relation to His creation i.e., all these matters either return to Him or are referred to Him and His disposal.

Derivative: مُّسْتَخْلَفِينَ from اِسْتَخْلَفَ – اِسْتِخْلاف which means to “leave something behind with someone and then leave them” and implies that the thing is temporary. مُّسْتَخْلَفis the one who has had something left behind with or for him. That which is left behind here is preceded by the words مِمَّا which is made up of مِن (from) and مَا (what) which is word that implies generality, with the meaning of “whatever”. As such everything that has been left to the believers is encompassed here which includes wealth, time and effort etc. They are therefore مُّسْتَخْلَفِينَ of all of these sources i.e., those who have inherited and been provided with them by Allah as trustees over them.

Derivative: رَءُوفٌ – In respect to humans رَأْفَة (Ra’fah) refers to the quality to be able to empathize with someone’s pain or identify with their situation and to be kind and compassionate about it. رَءُوفٌ (Ra’oof) in relation to Allah not only refers to His kindness but also to His being aware of what we face and go through and there to take care of us.
